Welcome, Walyson. You have finished eight lessons. How do you feel about your progress so far?
I feel good. I used to avoid the phone in English, but now I feel more confident.
That is wonderful. What is your main strength, and what do you still struggle with?
My strength is writing emails. I still struggle with speaking quickly, so I want to improve my fluency.
Good. My feedback is to review a little every week. What is your goal for the next block?
My goal is to speak in longer sentences and to overcome my fear of mistakes. I will reflect on my progress every month.
I used to feel nervous, but now I feel confident.
My biggest strength is writing emails.
I still struggle with speaking quickly.
My goal is to improve my fluency.
The feedback helped me overcome my fear of mistakes.

Hi Walyson, this is your teacher with a short progress note after our first eight lessons. I want you to reflect on how far you have come. When we started, you used to translate every sentence in your head, and you would pause for a long time before speaking. Now you sound much more confident, and you speak more fluently on the phone and in your emails. Your biggest strength is your vocabulary; you have learned a lot of useful business words. The main area to improve is the past tense, where you still struggle a little. My feedback is simple: keep a journal, review one lesson each week, and set a clear goal for the next block. You have worked very hard, and you should feel proud. Let us celebrate this progress and keep moving forward together.

This is my self-assessment journal for the first block of my English course. I want to reflect honestly on my progress. When I started, I used to feel nervous in every meeting, and I would avoid answering the phone in English. Now I feel more confident, and I can handle a call from a supplier without panic. My main strength is writing emails; I can write a clear message and choose the right words. My main weakness is speaking quickly, so I still need to improve my fluency. The feedback from my teacher helped me a lot. My goal for the next block is to speak in longer sentences and to overcome my fear of mistakes. I will review my notes every week, and I will keep moving forward.

First, look back at the lessons you have studied so far. Next, write down your main strengths. After that, be honest about your weaknesses. Then, ask your teacher for feedback. Finally, set a clear goal for the next block.
